sections.paragraph:
with suff. יִתְרֹו m.-

(1) a cord, a rope, prop. something hanging over, redundant, so called from hanging over, hanging down; see the root No.1. (Arab. وَتَرُ id. a bow string, harp string), Jud. 167 Jdg 16:7, seq. Specially-(a) used of the cords which in pitching a tent fasten the curtains to the stakes. Metaph. Job 4:21, נִסַּע יִתְרָם בָּם “their cords are torn away,” their tents are removed, i.e. they die (comp. the metaphor of a tent, verse 19 Job 4:19 Isa 38:12).-(b) of the string of a bow, Psa 11:2.-(c) of a cord used as a bridle; Job 30:11 כתיב, יִתְרֹו פִתַּח “he looses his bridle,” or coll. “they loose their bridle,” i.e. they are unbridled, unrestrained; קרי, יִתְרִי they loose my bridle, i.e. the rein which I put on them, or the reverence owed to me.

(2) abundance, Psa 17:14 Job 22:20 adv. abundantly, very, Isa 56:12 Dan 8:9 עַל יֶתֶר abundantly, enough and more, Psa 31:24.

(3) remainder, rest, residue; יֶתֶר הָעָם the rest of the people, Jud. 7:6 2Sa 10:10, יֶתֶר דִּבְרֵי שׁ׳ the rest of the acts of Solomon, 1Ki 11:41 compare 1Ki 14:19. But Joe 1:4, יֶתֶר הָאַרְבֶּה “that which the locust has left.”

(4) that which exceeds measure or limit, whence adv. besides (i.q. יֹותֵר 2, c); hence eminence, excellence, Pro 17:7. Concr. that which is excellent, or first, Gen 49:3.

(5) [Jether, Jethro], pr.n. m.-(a) Jud. 8:20 .-(b) 1Ch 2:32.-(c) 1Ch 4:17.-(d) 1Ch 7:38, for which there is יִתְרָן verse 37 1Ch 7:37.-(e) the father-in-law of Moses, elsewhere called יִתְרֹו Exo 4:18.-(f) 1Ki 2:5, for which there is יִתְרָא 2Sa 17:26. Patron. יִתְרִי 2Sa 23:38.
